subject: [RFC][PATCH] make sure that lock_for_kill() callers drop the locks in safe order

reviews.author: Paul McKenney
reviews.summary: reviewer noted that in a preempt-disabled kernel, the order of rcu_read_unlock() and spin_unlock() does not matter due to preemption being entirely disabled
sentiment_signals: no specific technical concern or request for change
reviews.raw_body:
Thank you, Jeff!

Yes that has preemption entirely disabled, so RCU readers do nothing but
disable and enable preemption.  Except that it is everywhere disabled,
so the order of the rcu_read_unlock() and the spin_unlock() should
not matter.  (Famous last words!)

							Thanx, Paul

2026-04-10.patch_summary: This patch ensures that callers of the lock_for_kill() function properly drop locks in a safe order to prevent potential deadlocks or hangs.
